properties.name: privacy.guard.enabled
properties.description: Master switch for the spring-privacy-guard starter.

properties.name: privacy.guard.fallback-mask-char
properties.description: Default mask token used when a field-level annotation does not provide a custom mask character.

properties.name: privacy.guard.logging.enabled
properties.description: Enables privacy-aware logging helpers and text sanitization.

properties.name: privacy.guard.logging.logback.install-turbo-filter
properties.description: Installs the Logback turbo filter automatically when Logback is on the classpath.

properties.name: privacy.guard.logging.logback.block-unsafe-messages
properties.description: Blocks unsafe log messages through the privacy Logback filter.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.enabled
properties.description: Enables audit publishers and audit service beans.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.log-events
properties.description: Writes sanitized audit events to the application log.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.repository-type
properties.description: Selects the backing repository implementation for audit persistence.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.async.enabled
properties.description: Publishes audit events asynchronously on a dedicated executor.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.async.thread-name-prefix
properties.description: Thread name prefix used by the built-in async audit executor.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.batch.enabled
properties.description: Buffers repository-backed audit events and flushes them in batches.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.batch.size
properties.description: Maximum number of buffered audit events to write in one batch.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.batch.flush-interval
properties.description: Maximum time an audit event stays buffered before a scheduled flush runs.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.retry.max-attempts
properties.description: Maximum publish or flush attempts before dead letter handling runs.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.retry.backoff
properties.description: Delay between retry attempts for async publishing and buffered flushes.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.repository-type
properties.description: Selects the repository used to persist exhausted audit retries.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.health.enabled
properties.description: Enables the dead-letter Actuator health indicator when Actuator is on the classpath.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.health.warning-threshold
properties.description: Marks the dead-letter health state as WARNING once the backlog reaches this threshold.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.health.down-threshold
properties.description: Marks the dead-letter health indicator as DOWN once the backlog reaches this threshold.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.metrics.enabled
properties.description: Enables the Micrometer gauges that report the current dead-letter backlog, thresholds, and active state tags.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.enabled
properties.description: Enables scheduled dead-letter threshold monitoring and callback delivery when alert callback beans are present.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.check-interval
properties.description: Controls how often the dead-letter backlog thresholds are evaluated for callback delivery.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.notify-on-recovery
properties.description: Emits a callback when the dead-letter backlog returns to CLEAR after a warning or down state.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.logging.enabled
properties.description: Registers the built-in logging callback for dead-letter threshold alerts.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.url
properties.description: Target webhook URL for the built-in dead-letter alert callback.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.bearer-token
properties.description: Optional bearer token sent with built-in dead-letter webhook alerts.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.signature-secret
properties.description: Optional HMAC secret used to sign built-in dead-letter webhook alerts.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.signature-algorithm
properties.description: HMAC algorithm used to sign built-in dead-letter webhook alerts.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.signature-header
properties.description: HTTP header that carries the built-in webhook alert signature.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.timestamp-header
properties.description: HTTP header that carries the built-in webhook alert timestamp.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.nonce-header
properties.description: HTTP header that carries the built-in webhook alert nonce.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.max-attempts
properties.description: Maximum number of delivery attempts for the built-in webhook alert callback.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.backoff
properties.description: Delay between built-in webhook alert retry attempts.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connect timeout for the built-in webhook alert HTTP client.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.webhook.read-timeout
properties.description: Request timeout for the built-in webhook alert callback.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.email.from
properties.description: Sender address for the built-in dead-letter email alert callback.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.email.to
properties.description: Recipient address for the built-in dead-letter email alert callback.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.email.subject-prefix
properties.description: Subject prefix used by the built-in dead-letter email alert callback.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.filter.enabled
properties.description: Registers the built-in Spring MVC webhook verification filter when enabled.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.filter.path-pattern
properties.description: Ant-style path pattern protected by the built-in webhook verification filter.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.interceptor.enabled
properties.description: Registers the built-in Spring MVC webhook verification interceptor when enabled.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.interceptor.path-pattern
properties.description: Ant-style path pattern protected by the built-in webhook verification interceptor.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.metrics.enabled
properties.description: Enables replay-store Micrometer gauges when a replay store bean is available.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.metrics.expiring-soon-window
properties.description: Window used to count replay-store entries that are about to expire.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.replay-store.jdbc.enabled
properties.description: Enables the JDBC-backed replay store for webhook receiver verification.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.replay-store.jdbc.table-name
properties.description: Target JDBC table used by the webhook receiver replay store.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.replay-store.jdbc.cleanup-interval
properties.description: Minimum interval between replay store cleanup passes.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.replay-store.jdbc.cleanup-batch-size
properties.description: Maximum number of expired replay-store entries removed per cleanup pass.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.replay-store.jdbc.initialize-schema
properties.description: Runs the built-in SQL schema before the JDBC replay store is used.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.replay-store.jdbc.schema-location
properties.description: Overrides the built-in replay store schema resource with a custom SQL script location.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.observability.alert.receiver.replay-store.jdbc.dialect
properties.description: Controls which built-in replay store SQL schema variant is selected for JDBC mode.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.jdbc.table-name
properties.description: Target JDBC table used by the dead letter repository.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.jdbc.initialize-schema
properties.description: Runs the built-in SQL schema before the dead letter JDBC repository is used.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.jdbc.schema-location
properties.description: Overrides the built-in dead letter schema resource with a custom SQL script location.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.dead-letter.jdbc.dialect
properties.description: Controls which built-in dead letter SQL schema variant is selected for JDBC mode.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.jdbc.table-name
properties.description: Target JDBC table used by the audit repository.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.jdbc.initialize-schema
properties.description: Runs the built-in SQL schema before the JDBC repository is used.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.jdbc.schema-location
properties.description: Overrides the built-in schema resource with a custom SQL script location.

properties.name: privacy.guard.audit.jdbc.dialect
properties.description: Controls which built-in SQL schema variant is selected for JDBC mode.

values.description: Store audit events in the in-memory repository for demos or tests.
Infer the schema dialect from the DataSource when possible.
Use the generic SQL schema resource.
Use the H2-specific SQL schema resource.
Use the MySQL-specific SQL schema resource.
Use the PostgreSQL-specific SQL schema resource.
